text:
Once upon a time in the land of Glenda, there lived a gentle fox named Felix. Felix had a small cave at the edge of a dense forest where he lived off berries and slept during the day. Every evening, he'd go back to his cave, ponder on his dreams of adventure, and gaze lovingly at the shimmering stars.

One quiet night, a mysterious owl named Owlet approached Felix's cave. Owlet was ancient, with hollow eyes that twinkled like stars. He had a way of listening that made every word seem important.

"Whisper tales to the wind," Owlet said one cool night. "The forest knows of quests long forgotten."

Felix listened intently, enchanted by the idea. He wondered what adventure could await him.

The following dawn, while the forest was barely stirring, a tiny light glowed on the other side of the great oak tree. Through the leaves, Felix could see a mouse—a mouse unlike any he'd ever seen.
